Jonathan Drack.description: Mauritian athlete (MRI) Jonathan Drack, described by commentators as probably Mauritius's top athlete and a finalist in last year's World Championships, enters with a determined look in a red tracksuit with yellow accents, illuminated by red smoke and a spotlight.

Nazim Babayev.description: Azerbaijani athlete (AZE) Nazim Babayev, described by commentators as an up-and-coming junior who won the European Junior title last year, walks through the tunnel with a focused expression in a red tracksuit with white pants, highlighted by red and purple lighting.

Alphonso Jordan.description: American athlete (USA) Alphonso Jordan, introduced by commentators as one of three Americans participating, walks confidently through the tunnel in a black tracksuit and white cap, accompanied by blue smoke and audience attention.

Tosin Oke.description: Nigerian athlete (NGR) Tosin Oke, an experienced competitor who previously represented Britain and was European Junior champion in 1999 according to commentators, enters with a composed demeanor in a green tracksuit, flanked by blue smoke effects and crowd cheers.

Olu Olamigoke.description: Nigerian athlete (NGR) Olu Olamigoke, introduced by commentators as second in the African Games with a personal best just under 17 meters, enters confidently in a white tracksuit and cap, accompanied by purple smoke and audience applause, marking the start of world-class triple jumping.

Harold Corréa.description: French athlete (FRA) in a dark blue tracksuit, entering with a confident stride, accompanied by purple smoke and audience attention. The commentator mentions he placed 'fifth in the European Indoors' held in Prague last March.

Max Hess.description: German athlete (GER) in a black and red tracksuit, entering with a focused expression, highlighted by blue and purple smoke effects. The commentator describes him as 'a very talented youngster'.

Marian Oprea.description: Romanian athlete (ROU) in a blue and white tracksuit, walking with a composed stride, highlighted by blue and purple lighting. The commentator describes him as 'the oldest in the field, very experienced, three times European Indoor medalist' and 'five times a top eight placer in the World Indoors'.

Roman Valiyev.description: Kazakh athlete (KAZ) in a gray hoodie and matching pants, entering with a relaxed posture, surrounded by green smoke and audience applause. The commentator notes he has won 'no less than six' Asian Indoor titles.

Benjamin Compaoré.description: French athlete (FRA) in a navy blue 'FRANCE' tracksuit, walking with arms outstretched, flanked by blue smoke and crowd cheers. The commentator introduces him as an 'Olympic finalist, European Champion two years ago'.

Nelson Évora.description: Portuguese athlete (POR) wearing a white and red tracksuit, entering with a wave and focused expression, illuminated by purple and red smoke effects. The commentator identifies him as the 'European Indoor Champion, former Olympic Champion' from Portugal, who won his Olympic title in 2008.

Chris Benard.description: American athlete (USA) in a black 'USA' t-shirt and black pants, walking with a determined look, illuminated by blue and red lighting. The commentator highlights his 'third' place in the US Championships and a personal best of 'over 17 meters outdoors'.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide shot of the sandpit at the IAAF World Indoor Championships in Portland 2016 for the Men's Triple Jump Final, as two male commentators begin their broadcast. Officials in green vests and white shirts are seen meticulously raking the sand smooth with long-handled brooms, a task praised by the commentators as 'a fabulous job' in preparing the landing area. Simultaneously, an on-screen graphic displays the event title and the scrolling list of 16 competitors, starting with Pablo Torrijos (ESP), Olumide Olamigoke (NGR), and Nazim Babayev (AZE), with the commentators detailing the 'lineup' and highlighting key athletes like former World Champion Nelson Evora of Portugal, Omar Craddock of the USA, and mentioning the absence of Christian Taylor and Pedro Picardo. The camera then focuses on the continued sandpit preparation before cutting to a dramatic, darkened arena where a large, illuminated 'TRIPLE JUMP MEN' sign glows in golden lights. The atmosphere shifts as upbeat, rock-style music, identified by the commentators as an 'inspired choice' and 'Van Halen jump,' swells. The scene transitions to a darkened tunnel entrance, dramatically lit by a spotlight and purple smoke effects, from which Pablo Torrijos (ESP) emerges confidently onto the field with arms raised, greeted by intensifying cheers from the crowd as a commentator announces, 'There is Pablo Torrijos...' The commentators engage in lighthearted banter about the music, calling it a 'perfect intro' for the night, as the first athlete makes his high-energy entrance.

The video opens dramatically in a darkened arena, illuminated by vibrant purple and red lighting and smoke effects, with a prominent golden 'TRIPLE JUMP MEN' sign setting the stage for the Men's Triple Jump event. The atmosphere is electric with crowd applause and a commentator introducing each athlete as they make their grand entrance through a smoke-filled tunnel. First, Pablo Torrijos (ESP), the Spanish champion and European indoor silver medalist from last year, strides in wearing a red and yellow tracksuit with arms raised, bathed in spotlight and purple smoke. He is followed by Olu Olamigoke (NGR), second in the African Games with a personal best just under 17 meters, entering confidently in a white tracksuit and cap amidst purple smoke. Next, Nazim Babayev (AZE), an up-and-coming junior and last year's European Junior title winner, appears in a red tracksuit with white pants, highlighted by red and purple lighting. Tosin Oke (NGR), an experienced competitor who was European Junior champion in 1999, enters composedly in a green tracksuit with blue smoke. Yordany Duranona (DMA), originally Cuban and fourth in the Pan Ams, walks through in a black hoodie and gray pants, surrounded by blue and purple smoke. Jonathan Drack (MRI), described as Mauritius's top athlete and a World Championships finalist last year, enters with a determined look in a red tracksuit with yellow accents, illuminated by red smoke. Finally, Alphonso Jordan (USA), one of three Americans participating, walks confidently in a black tracksuit and white cap, accompanied by blue smoke. Throughout these entrances, an on-screen graphic displays the competitor list with bib numbers, names, national flags, and country codes, enhancing the sense of anticipation for the upcoming competition.

The video captures a dramatic athlete entrance for the Men's Triple Jump Final, set against a backdrop dominated by a large, glowing 'TRIPLE JUMP MEN' sign. A darkened tunnel is brought to life with vibrant purple, red, blue, and green smoke, dynamic spotlights, and an energetic background music track, all accompanied by enthusiastic crowd cheers and a male commentator's voice. Nelson Évora (POR) is the first to emerge in a white and red tracksuit, waving, as the commentator introduces him as the "European Indoor Champion, former Olympic Champion" from Portugal, who secured his Olympic title in 2008. Marian Oprea (ROU) follows in a blue and white tracksuit, walking with a composed stride, identified by the commentator as "the oldest in the field, very experienced, three times European Indoor medalist" and a "five times a top eight placer in the World Indoors." Roman Valiyev (KAZ) then enters in a gray hoodie, with the commentator highlighting his achievement of "no less than six" Asian Indoor titles. Benjamin Compaoré (FRA) makes his entrance in a navy 'FRANCE' tracksuit, arms outstretched, introduced as an "Olympic finalist, European Champion two years ago." He is quickly followed by fellow Frenchman Harold Corréa (FRA) in a dark blue tracksuit, noted for placing "fifth in the European Indoors" held in Prague last March. Chris Benard (USA) appears next in a black 'USA' t-shirt, with the commentator detailing his "third" place in the US Championships and a personal best of "over 17 meters outdoors." The sequence concludes with Max Hess (GER) entering in a black and red tracksuit, described as "a very talented youngster," as the electric atmosphere and audience engagement create a high-stakes, celebratory ambiance for the competition.

event_blocks.summary: The IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland 2016 Men's Triple Jump commenced with dramatic athlete entrances through a tunnel adorned with 'TRIPLE JUMP MEN' signage, smoke, and spotlights. Bin Dong of China, the world leader at 17.41m, emerged first, followed by Omar Cradock of the USA, the reigning US champion, who waved to the crowd. Olu Olamigoke of Nigeria then prepared for his attempt, with background commentary noting a 16.16m jump by Tadios of Spain. Tosin Oke of Nigeria, an experienced athlete, executed a powerful hop, step, and jump, achieving a season's best of 16.73m, which initially placed him in the lead. Alphonso Jordan of the USA, invited due to relaxed qualifying standards, recorded a jump of 16.11m. Marian Oprea of Romania fouled on his takeoff, resulting in a 16.05m jump. Benjamin Compaoré of France then took the lead with an impressive 16.77m jump, building on his past successes as a European Champion. Chris Benard of the USA followed, with commentators deeming his jump 'rather lackluster' at 16.14m despite his apparent satisfaction. Bin Dong then delivered an 'excellent' jump with 'beautiful technique,' matching his personal best and Asian record of 17.41m, solidifying his position. Throughout the event, officials in green vests managed the sandpit, raking it between jumps, while event branding from 'SEIKO,' 'Portland 2016,' 'MONDO,' and 'Canon' was visible in the brightly lit arena filled with a murmuring crowd.

Omar Cradock.description: American athlete (USA) wearing a red sleeveless top, black pants, and a white cap, entering with a charismatic wave to the crowd, bib number 377 displayed on-screen, later seen removing warm-up pants and holding a white towel. Commentary identifies him as 'Omar Cradock,' 'twice an NCAA champion,' 'the US champion of last year,' 'the IF Tour winner this year,' and 'twice the American collegiate champion,' noting he is 'making the most of his introductions to the crowd' and 'following in great footsteps.'

Event Signage.description: Large, illuminated 'IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland 2016' branding on trackside barriers, along with sponsor logos such as 'SEIKO', 'Canon', and 'KGW8', visible throughout the segment.

IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland.description: Event branding visible on the trackside barriers, including logos for 'SEIKO', 'KGW8', and 'The Oregonian', indicating the official competition context.

Olu Olamigoke.description: Nigerian athlete (NGR) wearing a green and white Nigeria singlet with bib number 393, standing focused and composed on the track before beginning a warm-up jog, with 'SEIKO' and 'Portland 2016' visible on his bib. Commentary identifies him as 'Olamagoki of Nigeria' and states he is 'about to go in the Triple Jump,' with the commentator wondering about his performance.

Officials.description: Staff in green vests and white shirts visible near the track, managing equipment and assisting athletes, including one raking the sandpit area with a long-handled broom.

Bin Dong.description: Chinese athlete (CHN) in a red 'CHINA' singlet with 'SEIKO' and 'Portland 2016' branding, bib number 150, introduced by commentators as 'China's Bin Dong' and 'the world leader,' with his 'Asian record' of '17.41' meters mentioned. He is seen performing preparatory movements, sprinting, executing a powerful triple jump, and walking away after a 17.41m jump, matching his PB, which draws enthusiastic commentary about his 'excellent speed' and 'beautiful technique.'

On-Screen Results Graphic.description: Digital overlay displaying athlete's bib number, name, country flag, country code, jump distances (PB/SB), and current rank, appearing during and after each jump, including Benard's 16.14m and Dong's 17.41m PB.

Tosin Oke.description: Nigerian athlete (NGR) in a green singlet with 'NIGERIA', 'SEIKO', and 'PORTLAND' branding, bib number 295, performing powerful triple jumps with focused intensity. He is introduced by the commentator as "Tosin Oke" and later as "the very experienced Oke," with his jump being praised as "much the best jump we've seen" and getting "closer towards the 17-meter area." The commentator notes his nationality switch from Britain and describes his board hit as "pretty much perfectly, 6 centimeters to spare," concluding it was "a good opener for him at 16 meters 73." He is seen landing in the sandpit and walking away while checking his on-screen results displayed.

Alphonso Jordan.description: Alphonso Jordan, the American athlete (USA) in a red 'USA' singlet with 'SEIKO' and 'Portland 2016' branding, bib number 385, is introduced by an announcer who notes his limited indoor season performance and that he was invited to 'make up the numbers' due to relaxed qualifying standards. He is seen preparing with focused intensity, then sprints down the runway with powerful strides audible on the track, transitioning into the hop, step, and jump phases before a distinct thud as he lands in the sandpit. An announcer speculates his jump might be '16.20 maybe?' before the on-screen results confirm his jump distance of '16.11' meters, ranking 3rd. He walks away from the pit, checking his performance.

Marian Oprea.description: Romanian athlete (ROU) in a blue 'ROMANIA' singlet with 'SEIKO' branding, bib number 323, performing a triple jump with a 16.05m result, reacting with a bow and a look of disappointment after landing in the sandpit. The commentator identifies him by name, notes his consistent form, but describes this jump as 'not a very good opener' and 'only just over 16 meters', confirming the '1605' result and explaining the foul as 'behind the board'.

Benjamin Compaoré.description: French athlete (FRA) in a navy blue 'FRANCE' singlet with 'SEIKO' and 'Portland 2016' branding, bib number 203, performing a triple jump with focused intensity, landing in the sandpit, and celebrating with a raised arm; his result of 16.77m is displayed on-screen, placing him first. Commentators describe his jump as 'excellent' and confirm his 16.77m distance, noting it takes the lead. They also provide historical context, mentioning his 6th place at previous World Indoor Championships and Olympics, and his European Champion title in 2014.

Chris Benard.description: American athlete (USA) in a red 'USA' singlet with 'SEIKO' and 'Portland 2016' branding, bib number 369, seen landing in the sandpit, celebrating with raised arms, and walking away after a 16.14m jump, a result confirmed by commentators.

event_blocks.summary: Chinese athlete Bin Dong, in a red 'CHINA' singlet, executed a superb triple jump, described as 'very straight, lovely leg shoot at the end,' landing in the sandpit for an early leading mark of 17.18, despite being 'miles behind the board.' American athlete Omar Cradock, wearing a red 'USA' sleeveless top and sunglasses, entered dramatically, gesturing to the cheering crowd before his attempt. While a 'powerful man,' his jump was noted as 'good but not as far as Dong,' and he appeared to be 'enjoying life' despite not fully liking the result. The young Azerbaijani athlete Nazim Babayev, the European Junior Champion, fouled his jump, indicated by an 'X' on the on-screen graphic, leaving him visibly disappointed. Tosin Oke of Nigeria then achieved a valid jump of 16.65m, improving slightly on his personal best, with commentators analyzing his technique, noting he ran 'on the edge of the run-up' and took off 'on the white part of the tape,' veering 'towards his left.' Jonathan Drack of Mauritius, based in France, prepared for his attempt amidst the vibrant atmosphere of the IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland 2016, with event signage and sponsor logos like 'SEIKO' and 'adidas' visible, and officials in green vests managing the competition.

A commentator describes him as a "powerful man" and notes his jump as "good" but "not as far as Dong," observing that he "doesn't entirely like it" but is "enjoying life."

event_blocks.summary: At the IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland 2016, American Omar Cradock (bib 377), wearing a red 'USA' singlet, sunglasses, and a gold chain, prepared for his triple jump, needing over 17 meters for a medal, with his outdoor personal best at 17.53 meters. event_blocks.summary: Chinese triple jumper Bin Dong, wearing a red 'CHINA' singlet, secured the gold medal at the IAAF Triple Jump Men event in Portland 2016 with a winning jump of 17.33 meters, having previously achieved leading jumps of 17.18 and 17.29, and a world-leading 17.41. Despite a subsequent foul, his victory was confirmed, prompting a broad smile and a celebratory sprint. He later removed his singlet, revealing a muscular physique, and was warmly congratulated by French athlete Benjamin Compaoré, who held a bouquet, and German athlete Max Hess, who secured silver with a personal best of 17.14 meters. Compaoré took bronze with 17.09 meters. Bin Dong received his own bouquet from an official amidst prominent 'SEIKO', 'TDK', 'adidas', and 'Canon' branding, with a photographer capturing the moments. The on-screen graphic confirmed Bin Dong as the winner, followed by Hess and Compaoré, listing all 16 competitors in the packed arena under bright lights.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Chinese triple jump athlete Bin Dong, in a red 'CHINA' singlet with 'SEIKO' and 'DONG' branding, standing focused on the green track. Commentators immediately highlight his leading position, stating he has "won this competition" and listing his impressive jumps of "17.18, 17.29, 17.33," noting he is the "world leader at 17 meters 41." An on-screen graphic confirms his best jump of 17.33 meters. Bin Dong then smiles broadly, a "lovely smile" according to the commentator, before beginning a powerful sprint. He executes a clean triple jump with strong form, landing in the sandpit, though the commentator notes "it was over, red flag raised," calling it "a bit of an anti-climax." Despite the foul on that specific jump, his victory is confirmed, with the commentator declaring "China's gold medal to Dong Bin in the triple jump. Dong takes the gold medal." Bin Dong raises his arms in celebration, then removes his singlet, revealing a muscular physique, and walks towards officials and other athletes. The on-screen graphic updates to show him as the 'WINNER' of the IAAF Triple Jump Men event at Portland 2016 with 17.33 meters. He is warmly greeted by Benjamin Compaoré, the French athlete in a navy blue 'FRANCE' singlet, who is holding a bouquet of flowers. Bin Dong then receives his own bouquet from an official, smiling and acknowledging the crowd amidst event branding for 'SEIKO', 'TDK', 'adidas', and 'Canon'. Commentators laud his performance, exclaiming "What a series of jumps by Bin Dong!" and praising his "such speed on the runway," while also announcing that "Hess takes the silver" and "Compaoré takes the bronze." A photographer with a large camera on a tripod captures the victorious moments.

The segment opens on the celebratory post-competition scene of the Men's Triple Jump Final at the IAAF World Indoor Championships Portland 2016. Commentators are heard discussing a potential personal best, then providing historical context, noting that the only Chinese male to win an indoor gold medal previously was Liu Xiang in the hurdles. Visually, Bin Dong, the Chinese winner, is prominent, shirtless and in red shorts, holding a bouquet of red roses as he walks along the green track, smiling and shaking hands with fellow competitors. The commentator confirms, "The triple jump, the gold medal went to Dong Bin of China at 17:33." He warmly greets Max Hess of Germany, who also holds flowers, as the commentator announces, "The silver, massive personal best for Max Hess of Germany at 17:14." Bin Dong then interacts with Benjamin Compaoré of France, also with flowers, with the commentator adding, "And the bronze to Benjamin Compaoré of France at 17:09." Officials in green vests are visible, one handing flowers to Bin Dong. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the packed arena under bright overhead lights, showcasing trackside branding for 'SEIKO', 'Canon', 'TDK', and 'adidas', along with 'Portland 2016' signage. A prominent on-screen results graphic displays Bin Dong in first place with 17.33 meters, followed by Max Hess (17.14m, PB) and Benjamin Compaoré (17.09m, SB), listing all 16 competitors including Tosin Oke, Pablo Torrijos, Nazim Babayev, and Harold Corréa. The atmosphere is clearly celebratory, with athletes and officials moving around the track and sandpit area.
